rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no monetary amounts present.

rows.description: Computus Rectoris a 2do die Novembris A D. 1859 ad eundem diem revolutum A.D. 1860
rows.notes: Long Latin header directly under the year; handwriting legible though some abbreviations ('2do', 'A D.') required interpretation; no amounts.
Centered section heading (handwritten 'Receptra' / 'Receipts'); no amounts in this row.
Single number '10' clearly aligned under the pounds column. Surname reads as 'Clutterbuck' in the handwriting; small uncertainty in exact spelling of name.
Number '30' clearly under pounds column. Abbreviations '4 do. on so.' interpreted as written; minor ambiguity in punctuation only.
Two small numbers present in the right columns interpreted as 8 shillings and 6 pence (no pounds figure written). Alignment with shilling and pence rules validated (8 < 20, 6 < 12).

Large value '1308' is clearly in pounds column with '6' and '7' aligned in shillings and pence. Handwriting of description is moderately dense but readable as Colby/Bursar/Income; slight uncertainty in the exact short words.
Pounds '1986' clearly aligned; shillings column shows '0' and pence '7'. The description uses ledger shorthand ('Dr. do. do.') which is typical; transcription kept as-is.
Numbers '77 13 4' clearly written and well aligned under pounds/shillings/pence columns. Description legible.

rows.description: Div. on Mr. Jones consols recd. in '58
rows.notes: Amounts read as '17 2 9' under pounds/shillings/pence. Description is abbreviated ('Div. on Mr. Jones consols') — interpretation of 'recd. in '58' added to make sense of the note; moderate handwriting abbreviation ambiguity.
Clear numeric trio '154 13 9' in the three money columns. The word after 'Fund' is faint but read as 'Ant.' or similar; annotated as (Closed) to reflect truncated handwriting.

rows.description: Late Building Fund - not carried to acct.
rows.notes: Pounds '47' and shillings '1' are visible; pence column looks blank or faint (interpreted as 0). The phrase 'not carried to acct.' is transcribed from the handwriting but is somewhat cramped and faint.
Single '10' appears in the pounds column aligned with this description. The name 'Richards' and 'Divinity Prize' are legible; minor uncertainty in first name/title but entry clear.

Amounts '11 8 6' are visible and aligned. The first word of description is somewhat ambiguous in the handwriting (reads as 'Rype' or possibly 'Hope'/'Type'); preserved as seen, with moderate uncertainty.
Clear numeric sequence '738 11 8' under pounds/shillings/pence. Description 'Sixteen per cent on Rents' is legible and matches context; high confidence.
Amounts read as '332 10' with the pence column blank (interpreted as 0). Description is clear though somewhat abbreviated; columns align well.

rows.description: Overplus Room Rent Xmas 1858 & do 1859 Rents
rows.notes: Numbers '7 11' visible in pounds and shillings; pence not written (interpreted as 0). The phrase 'Xmas' and the years are deciphered from faint script; moderate confidence.

rows.description: Repayment of money borrowed by Rector from Cantin Fund (Principal £30; Interest 7s 4d)
rows.notes: This appears as a bracketed pair of figures (principal 30 and interest ~7/4/5) summing to '37 4 5' which is written in the money columns. The description is inferred from condensed handwriting and the visible '30' and smaller interest figures; moderate uncertainty in exact phrasing but numeric sum is readable.
Single '9' in pounds column aligned with this line. Description is legible ('Repayment from Choir Fund'), small uncertainty only in preceding article 'The'.

Large number '1910 19 8' clearly in the three currency columns. The description is slightly abbreviated/contracted in the handwriting ('2000 Consols sale of Cantor Fund' interpreted from script); numeric reading is clear and validated (shillings < 20, pence < 12).
Small '1' in pounds column aligned with this short description; name 'Hammond' and 'Key' are legible though the surname could be marginally ambiguous.
Amount '5' in pounds column is clear. The name in the description is somewhat faint and could be 'Allnutt'/'Altnutt' — transcribed as seen with moderate uncertainty.

Amount '30' in pounds column is clear. The description contains abbreviations and is somewhat cramped but interpreted as a sub-rector repayment related to choir instructions; moderate confidence in text.
Final total '6726 11 7' is prominently written and well aligned under the three money columns. Very clear numerals; description cell is blank on the page.
